PL: Hm, jak sobie życzysz. Poszedłem do jej tymczasowej kryjówki, żeby sprawdzić, jak idą przygotowania do zawarcia umowy, ale kiedy dotarłem na miejsce, wszystko było przetrząśnięte do czysta. Były też ślady walki.
EN: Hmm, as you wish. I was going to her temporary lodgings to check up on her progress for the deal, however when I had arrived the place was swept clean. Signs of a struggle.

PL: Znalazłem tę przypinkę. Jak widzisz, aż bije od niej kultem.
EN: Found this pin - as you can see, cult presence written all over it.

PL: Tak, te pajęcze motywy nie są zbyt subtelne.
EN: Yeah, couldn't be more subtle with their spider motifs.

PL: Racja. Miałem zabrać sprzęt i ruszyć ich tropem, ale cóż, tutejsi nie przepadają za nowymi twarzami.
EN: Indeed. Was going to get my supplies and track them further, but well, folk round here don't take kindly to new arrivals it seems.
also a bit mockingly
Should have chosen a better disguise then.
a bit irritated from all the mock

PL: Nie pouczaj mnie o szpiegostwie, wiedźminie. Akurat ty nie potrafisz nie zwracać uwagi.
EN: Don't lecture me on the art of espionage witcher, you can't even pass by without catching the eye of every person in the vicinity.

PL: W każdym razie, wróć do Arette i rusz głównym traktem. Od tego zacznij. Może złapiesz ich ślad dzięki tym swoim wiedźmińskim sztuczkom. Łatwo nie będzie, to główna droga, pełno tam tropów.
EN: In any case, you should head back to Arette, catch the main road. You can start from there. Could be able to pick up their trail with your witcher senses. Though it may prove difficult, it's the main road after all, lots of tracks to pick from.
Anything else?
What about her house? Where is it?
Anything else I should know?

PL: Hmm... Powiedziałem ci już wszystko, co najważniejsze.
EN: Hmm... I've told you the important bits you need to know already, so no.

PL: Dobrze. A co z jej domem? Gdzie on jest?
EN: Right, and what about her house? Where is it?

PL: Zamurze w Novigradzie. Na co ci on? Już mówiłem. Nie ma tam niczego poza zakrwawioną podłogą i porozrzucanymi krzesłami.
EN: Farcorners in Novigrad. Why do you care? Told you already what I found there, not much to look at but a bloody floor and flung chairs.
This small scene is taken from the game, so it should be translated and voiced in the PL strings.
Maybe, maybe not.

PL: Skoro wolisz tracić czas, zamiast wykonywać instrukcje od zleceniodawcy, proszę bardzo. Ja nie biorę za to odpowiedzialności.
EN: If you choose to waste your time instead of doing actual work, be my guest. I shall not be responsible for your failure if it comes to that.

PL: Nie pouczaj mnie o byciu wiedźminem. Chyba szef cię szuka.
EN: You just run along to your master, don't worry about what I do.
a bit threatening

PL: Hmm... Stąpasz po cienkim lodzie, wiedźminie. Dobrze ci radzę, rób, co ci każą, a wszystko pójdzie gładko. Nie utrudniaj sobie życia.
EN: Hmm... you thread on thin ice, witcher. Take my advice - do as you're told, and things will go smoothly. Don't make things more difficult for yourself.
If you say so.
I bid you farewell, and good luck.

PL: Legendarny Biały Wilk, mniema, że jest dość wielki, by stanąć na drodze Coram Agh Tera. Cóż za bezczelność.
EN: The legendary White Wolf, thinking himself great enough to stand in the way of Coram Agh Tera. Such foolishness.

PL: Jesteś tylko kundlem spuszczonym ze smyczy, błachostką.
EN: A rabid lapdog off of its leash, nothing more than a nuisance.

PL: Myślisz, że jesteś bystry, wiedźminie? Że tak świetnie to rozgryzłeś? Nic nie wiesz...
EN: You think yourself smart, witcher? To have figured this all out? You know nothing...

PL: Dosyć tego! Koniec gierek, wiedźminie. To jest twój koniec!
EN: Enough of this! No more games, witcher. It ends NOW!

PL: Żałosne. I ty się masz za mistrza miecza.
EN: Pathetic! And you call yourself a sword master.

PL: Twoje wiedźmińskie sztuczki ci nie pomogą, wilku.
EN: Your witcher tricks won't do you any good here, wolf.

PL: Uciekaj, unikaj, bez różnicy. Ofiara zawsze kończy w pajęczej sieci.
EN: Run, evade, makes no difference. The spider's web catches all eventually.

PL: Dosyć tego! Koniec gierek, wiedźminie. To jest twój koniec!
EN: Enough of this! No more games, witcher. It ends NOW!

PL: Żałosne. I ty się masz za mistrza miecza.
EN: Pathetic! And you call yourself a sword master.

PL: Twoje wiedźmińskie sztuczki ci nie pomogą, wilku.
EN: Your witcher tricks won't do you any good here, wolf.

PL: Uciekaj, unikaj, bez różnicy. Ofiara zawsze kończy w pajęczej sieci.
EN: Run, evade, makes no difference. The spider's web catches all eventually.

PL: Więc... Nadszedł czas... Pan... Przecina moje nici... Mój cel... Został osiągnięty.
EN: So... my times has come... My master... cuts my threads... My purpose has been... fulfilled.
Good sir! Help us, please!
What's going on?!
She is talking to the corpse of her friend
Erden... hold on, just a bit longer!

PL: Obawiam się, że już za późno. Nie żyje.
EN: Afraid, he can't wait even a moment longer. He's dead.

PL: Co...? Nie... Przed chwilą jeszcze oddychał!
EN: What...? No... he was alive a moment ago.

PL: Musiał odejść przed chwilą. Przykro mi.
EN: Died in the next moment then. I'm sorry, nothing I can do.
Erden...
What happened here, exactly?
And you are...?
What happened here, exactly?

PL: Ja i Erden... Szliśmy w stronę naszego miejsca... Pod tamto drzewo. A potem...
EN: Me and Erden... we were going to out usual spot. Under the tree, over there. And then...
Were you a friend of his or...?

PL: Ja... Eee... Tak. B-bardzo bliską... Przyjaciółką.
EN: I... uh, uhm, yes. A v-very good... friend.

PL: C-coś wyszło z lasu i podeszło do nas. Z daleka wyglądało jak człowiek, ale z bliska jak trup. Dźgnęło Erdena mieczem i odeszło.
EN: S-something came out of the woods, approached us. Looked like a human at first. It wasn't. Up close it looked like a corpse that came back from the dead. It stabbed Erden with it's sword and then left.

PL: Odeszło? Tak po prostu? Nie zauważyło cię?
EN: It left? Just like that? Didn't see you?

PL: Chyba mnie widziało, ale nawet nie spojrzało w moją stronę... Ja... Stałam obok Erdena, kiedy go to zaatakowało!
EN: No, it did see me, I was next to Erden when he got stabbed. It didn't even look at me though, as if I wasn't there.

PL: Hmm. Co najmniej dziwne. Dobrze, że jesteś cała.
EN: Hmm. Pretty strange to say the least. At least you didn't get hurt as well.
Say, in which direction did it go after it left.

PL: Tam. P-proszę, zabij to, mistrzu wiedźminie. Pomścij Erdena.
EN: That way. P-please kill it, master witcher. Avenge Erden.

PL: Taki mam plan. Coś mi mówi, że to może mieć związek z pewną inną sprawą. W innym przypadku, byłby to bardzo dziwny zbieg okoliczności.
EN: Don't need any convincing, something tells me this might be connected to the people I'm after. Or a pretty big coincidence that it happened right here.

PL: Tak czy inaczej, powinnaś już stąd iść.
EN: Anyhow, you should get going.

PL: Tak... Muszę zadbać o pochówek Erdena. Nie mogę go tak zostawić...
EN: Yes... should arrange a burial for Erden, can't leave him like this...
What do we have here?

PL: Hmm, to wygląda jak ożywione zwłoki...
EN: Hmm, it does look like a corpse that came back to life...

PL: Ciało i ubrania wyglądają na spalone. Może to jest powiązane?
EN: Body and clothes look burned. A connection, maybe?
Weird ""necklace"" on his neck.

PL: Nie wygląda na bestię z lasu, więc co tutaj robi?
EN: Doesn't look like a monster you'd find in the forest. What's it doing here?

PL: Niczego więcej się tu nie dowiem.
EN: Won't learn more here.

PL: Ciało i odzienie wyglądają na spopielone. Zupełnie jakby przez moment zajęły się żywym ogniem.
EN: Body and clothes look scorched. Almost as if it burst into flames for a moment.

PL: Czy to może być powiązane ze śladami ognia, które znalazłem wcześniej?
EN: Could it be connected to the burn mark I found earlier?

PL: Wielce groteskowy naszyjnik. Zastanawia mnie, dlaczego to coś miało go na sobie.
EN: Pretty grotesque necklace. Wonder why it has it on.

PL: To nie jest typowe leśne stworzenie, co ono tu robi?
EN: Not your typical forest being, is it? Wonder what's it doing here.

PL: Zbłądziło czy coś je tu zwabiło?
EN: Did it wonder off here, or was it attracted by something?
Unnatural burns, humanoid, wearing a "necklace"...

PL: Wałęsający się po okolicy, w której być go nie powinno... Czy to skutek jakiejś klątwy? Ciekawe.
EN: Wondering in a place it shouldn't be... Did a curse turn him into this? Curious.
Won't learn any more here.
[Use the Eye of Nehaleni.]
[Use the Seeing Stone.]
Eye here refers to The Eye of Nehaleni

PL: I nic? Dziwne. Wychodzi na to, że Nilfgaardczyk miał rację. Przy tej iluzji Oko Nehaleni mi nie pomoże.
EN: Nothing...? Weird, guess the nilfgaardian was right. This illusion clearly doesn't respond to the Eye.

PL: Kult nie chce żeby ktoś z zewnątrz odnalazł ten dom. Muszę być ostrożny.
EN: The cult definitely didn't want outsiders to find this house. Should be careful, though.

PL: Krew jest mocno zaschnięta, chociaż niektóre plamy są świeższe, wnosząc po ich odcieniu. Wleczono tędy wiele ofiar.
EN: Blood's old. Though it seems it's mixed with some newer splatters, judging by the color. Guess a lot of victims got dragged through here.

PL: Hmm, ciekawe czy to pierwsi właściciele tego domu.
EN: Hmm, wonder if those were the original owners of the house.

PL: Świeży ślad. Kogoś tu ciągnięto. To mogła być Carilla.
EN: This trail's fresh. Someone was dragged through here recently. I'd guess it was Carilla.

PL: Krąg teleportacji. Pewnie prowadzi do ich kryjówki. Muszę znaleźć sposób, żeby go aktywować.
EN: A teleportation circle. Probably leading to their lair. Gotta find a way to open the portal.
What's this?
Odd as decorations go.
Bloody knife. Used a lot it seems.
A note?
Wonder what this is about.

PL: Zapalić świeczki, dotknąć czaszki i... Okazać oddanie. Co to znaczy?
EN: Light the candles and touch the skull... and showing my devotion. Wonder what that means.
Hmm.
[Examine closer.]
[Step away.]

PL: Krwawy odcisk dłoni na czaszce. Krew jeszcze świeża.
EN: Bloody hand print on the skull. Blood's fresh.
Hmm.

PL: To pewnie czaszka, o której mowa w notatce. Krwawy odcisk dłoni, świeży.
EN: This should be the skull mentioned in the note. Bloody handprint on it, fresh.

PL: Może w ten sposób okazują oddanie. Muszę zanurzyć rękę we krwi.
EN: Maybe that's what they mean to show devotion. Gotta bloody my hand.
Translation - could use a polish idiom if there is one close to this

PL: Patrzcie, kogo tu licho przywiało, chłopcy.
EN: Look at what the cat dragged in, boys.

PL: Ano, owieczka przyszła na własną rzeź. To ci cuda.
EN: Aye, lamb came to the slaughterhouse on it's own. 'Tis a miracle.
Maybe this is our blessing form master.

PL: No, chodź owieczko, miejmy to za sobą. Ołtarz jeszcze ciepły.
EN: C'mon lamb, let's get this over with, the sacrificial stone is still warm.
determined

PL: Nie musisz mi dwa razy powtarzać.
EN: You don't have to ask me twice.

PL: Iluzja skrywa wejście. Na szczęście mogę zdjąć czar poźniej, jak znajdę Carillę.
EN: An illusion hides the exit from the outside. Luckily I can dispell it later after I find Carilla.

PL: Ech, ale tu cuchnie... Ilu ludzi zabili ci zwyrodnialcy?
EN: Ugh, it reeks! Just how many people have those degenerates killed?
Uh, it worked.

PL: Nie spieszy im się zdychać. Szlag.
EN: They refuse to die. Shit.

PL: Musi być jakiś sposób, żeby je załatwić.
EN: There should be something I can do to stop them.

PL: Czaszka na beczce. Muszę ją zniszczyć.
EN: The skull on that barrel. Gotta break it!

PL: Jestem pewien, że gdzieś tutaj widziałem klucz.
EN: Pretty sure I saw the key around here.
Shit. He teleported out.

PL: A już myślałem, że będzie za łatwo...
EN: How about you play with this, witcher?
surprised but happy

PL: Co my tu mamy? Jakaż to muszka znalazła drogę do pajęczej sieci?
EN: O-oh, what do we have here? A fly finds her way into the spiderweb.

PL: Ech, wy, obłąkańcy i te wasze górnolotne przenośnie...
EN: Guess all of you lunatics like metaphors, go figure.
Release that woman. Now!

PL: Nie da rady, tobie też nie pozwolimy odejść. Jak się na to zapatrujesz?
EN: We can't do that, in fact, we won't let you leave either. How about that?
What will you do to her?
What's your goal?

PL: Gdybyście mieli ją złożyć w ofierze, nie siedziałaby teraz w klatce. Po co to wszystko?
EN: What's your goal here? Doesn't look like you'll sacrifice her straight away. Keeping her in a cage... What are you planning?

PL: Czego od niej chcecie? Nie złożyliście jej na ołtarzu. Dlaczego ją porwaliście?
EN: What'll you do to her? Keeping her in a cage and not killing her straight away. Why did you kidnap her, specifically?

PL: Patrzcie go, kurwa, detektyw się znalazł. Myślisz, że opowiemy ci wszystko, ot tak?
EN: Who the fuck do you think we are!? Think we'll tell you everything just like that?

PL: No chłopaki, jak myślicie, co powinniśmy z nim zrobić?
EN: What do you say boys? What should we do with him?
My blade's already itching to cut him up.

PL: Cierpliwości, to nie byle kto. Zastanawia mnie, jakie błogosławieństwo dostaniemy od naszego mistrza, za złożenie mu w ofierze wiedźmina...
EN: Patience. He's a witcher after all. Ah, I wonder what blessing will master grant us after offering him a witcher...
Come now mutant, how will you fare against this?
Hey, you alright?
She just woke up after being saved
Oh... uhm, yes I think so.

PL: Jesteś już bezpieczna. Nie musisz się niczego bać.
EN: You're safe now, don't have to worry about a thing.
S-safe... Yes. Th... thank you.

PL: Co się stało? Czego chcieli od ciebie kultyści?
EN: What happened? What did these cultists want with you?

PL: Osoba, którą mnie przysłała. Co możesz mi o nim powiedzieć?
EN: The person who sent me. What can you tell me about them?

PL: Co z twoimi dłońmi? To sprawka kultystów?
EN: What happened to your hands? Cultists did this to you?

PL: Spokojnie. Na pewno nic nie pamiętasz?
EN: Calm down, you sure you can't remember anything?

PL: Byłem w twoim domu, znalazłem tam kilka ciekawych przedmiotów.
EN: Been to your house, found some interesting items...

PL: Pójdę już umówić spotkanie z Nilfgaardczykami.
EN: You rest. I'll go arrange a meeting with the nilfgaardians as agreed.
This should be the one.

PL: No dobrze. Trzeba iść po Carillę i czekamy.
EN: All right. Now to go get Carilla and wait.
Janus to Geralt and Carilla, excited, almost mocking
Oh, he's here!
Carilla to Janus, almost confused at the start but goes back to normal with the "hello"
Janus... hello.

PL: Witaj, droga Carillo! Mam nadzieję, że podróż upłynęła przyjemnie, pomijając te perypetie z porwaniem, rzecz jasna.
EN: Hello, my dear Carilla! I hope you had a pleasant trip, without the whole kidnapping part. Hehe.

PL: Jest i także nasz bohater! Dobrze się spisałeś, wiedźminie. Wiedziałem, że sobie poradzisz.
EN: And of course, the man of the hour. Good work witcher, I knew you'd manage.

PL: Nie mam w zwyczaju nie dotrzymywać słowa. A skoro o tym mowa... Gdzie on jest?
EN: Agreed to help, after all. Gotta keep my word. Speaking of... where is he?

PL: To bardzo zajęty człowiek. Oczekiwałeś, że będzie się wlókł specjalnie taki kawał z Wyzimy?
EN: He's a busy man, you know. What? Did you expect him to come all the way here from Vizima?
Right...
Translation - not sure if there is a saying for lollygagging in Polish

PL: Dobra, my tu gadu gadu, a czas ruszać... Chodź, Carillo, jesteś już bezpieczna. Odprowadzimy cię. Pewnie jesteś zmęczona...
EN: Alright then, enough lollygagging, come Carilla, you're safe now. We shall escort you. You must need the rest.

PL: Twoja nagroda, wiedźminie. Zgodnie z umową, niezła sumka. Jesteś pewien, że nie użyłeś magii żeby się na nią zgodził? Haha.
EN: Your reward, witcher. A hefty sum you agreed on, you sure you didn't use some sorta magic to make him agree on it. Haha...

PL: To był oczywiście żart. Proszę. Chyba nie muszę dodawać, że, rzecz jasna, z wyrazami szacunku.
EN: I jest, of course. Here. With kind regards, this should go without saying.

PL: Oczywiście... Oszczędź sobie ten teatrzyk i płać. Nic tu więcej po mnie.
EN: Of course... Just cut the crap and give me the reward. I'm done here.

PL: Zatem żegnaj, Biały Wilku. Zaiste, serce bolesne dla mnie, że już się nie spotkamy - tak ciężko dziś o posłusznego czworonoga!
EN: Now I bid you farewell, White Wolf. We shall not meet again, sadly. I'm almost moved to tears... Good dogs are hard to come by! Hehe.

PL: Hmm... Mam wrażenie, że to wszystko poszło za łatwo. Może coś przeoczyłem?
EN: Hmm... Can't help but think I missed something here.

PL: Eh, było, minęło. Muszę się zająć swoimi sprawami.
EN: Eh, what's done is done. Got my own troubles to deal with.
Thank you...

PL: Nigdy cię nie zapomnę, ukochany... Zawsze będę nosić ten naszyjnik... Erden...
EN: I'll never forget you... for as long as I have this necklace on my neck... Erden...
confused but annoyed and a little bit angry at the same time

PL: Gdzie ja, u licha, jestem? Muszę znależć Arachosa i to zakończyć.
EN: Where am I? Gotta find Arachous, put an end to this.
Of course there's more!
Shit, an illusion! That was a close one.

PL: Kazałeś mi na siebie czekać, wiedźminie.
EN: Kept me waiting, witcher.
Arachous to Geralt, angry
You dare!

PL: Wdzianko odpowiednio dopasowane do okoliczności, co, Helphyrze? A może powinienem powiedzieć "Arachosie"?
EN: Dressed for the occasion, eh, Helphyr? Or should I say, Arachous?

PL: Nazywaj mnie jak chcesz, kundlu. Wydaje ci się, że jesteś sprytny, ale od początku tańczyłeś dokładnie tak, jak ci zagrałem. Wystarczy już tylko postawić kropkę nad i.
EN: Call me whatever you wish, dog. You may think yourself clever, but you did everything I asked of you and more. Now all that's left is for me to pick up the pieces.
Just try it.

PL: Głupcze, nawet nie masz pojęcia, z kim masz do czynienia. Ale zaraz to zrozumiesz...
EN: You fool. You have no idea who you are dealing with. But you shall now find out.

PL: Już po wszystkim, Arachos nie żyje.
EN: Its over. Arachous is dead.

PL: Dziękuję, Geralt. Za uwolnienie mnie od tego koszmaru.
EN: Thank you, Geralt. For freeing me from this nightmare.

PL: Dobrze, że mogłem pomóc. Myślałaś, co dalej?
EN: Glad I could help. What will you do now?

PL: Chyba wrócę do sióstr... Do Ellander. Tak. Wrócę do dawnego życia.
EN: I think I shall return to my sisters... In Ellander. Yes. Go back to my old life.
Plan to do just that.
Came across a curious scene while tracking you. Fought a cursed being...

PL: Weź kilka monet na drogę. Pozdrów ode mnie Nenneke.
EN: Here, some coin for the trip. Say hi to Nenneke for me.
Farewell and good luck.

PL: Kapłanki zapewne odchodzą od zmysłów. Masz tutaj kilka monet na drogę. I... Pozdrów ode mnie Nenneke.
EN: They're probably worried sick as is. Here, some coin for the trip. Say hi to Nenneke for me while you're at it.

PL: Znasz matkę Nenneke? Mogłam się domyślić, zawsze umiała otaczać się dobrymi duszami. Jeszcze raz ci dziękuję, Geralt.
EN: You know Mother Nenneke? I should have guessed, she always knew good souls to keep company with. Thank you again, Geralt.

PL: Żegnaj i powodzenia. Mam nadzieję, że jeszcze się zobaczymy.
EN: Farewell and good luck. I hope we meet again.

PL: Żegnaj, Biały Wilku. Powodzenia na szlaku.
EN: Farewell, White Wolf. Good luck on the path.

PL: Co się dokładnie stało? Czego chcieli od ciebie kultyści?
EN: What happened? What did these cultists want with you?

PL: Zostałam wysłana... By negocjować z gangami i... Nieludźmi. Wszystko szło dobrze... Dopóki nie przyszli.
EN: I... was sent... to negotiate terms with the gangs and... nonhumans. Everything was going smoothly... until they came.

PL: Czemu ty? O co im chodziło?
EN: Why target you? What was their goal?
I... I don't know.

PL: Mężczyzna, który cię szuka. Co o nim wiesz? Nie wygląda na zwykłego polityka.
EN: The person who sent me. What can you tell me about them? They don't look like any ordinary politician out for glory.

PL: Ja... On... On ma plany. Dla Nilfgaardu. Dla Północy. Wie, co jest dla nich najlepsze. Wysłał mnie, żeby... Negocjować... Zawiodłam...
EN: I... he... he's got plans. For Nilfgaard. For the North. He knows what's best. He sent me to... negotiate... I failed...
Not your fault.
He... will not be pleased.

PL: Twoje dłonie... Co się z nimi stało? To sprawka kultystów?
EN: Your hands... What happened to them? Cultists did this to you?

PL: Moje dłonie? Nie... Nie pamiętam. Może gdzieś upadłam?
EN: My hands...? I don't... recall. Maybe I fell somewhere?

PL: To wygląda jak... Ślady po ugryzieniach.
EN: Dunno. Seem like... bite marks.

PL: Ugryzienia... Nie. Blizny... Tak... Chyba tak. Małe... Drobne blizny. Po ugryzieniach...
EN: Bites... no. Scars... Yes... I think so. Small... tiny scars. Bite scars...
Hmm...

PL: Już dobrze. Jesteś bezpieczna. Na pewno nic nie pamiętasz?
EN: Calm your mind. You're safe. Are you sure you can't remember?

PL: Ja... Nic nie pamiętam. Czegoś chcieli... Ode mnie... Od niego... Już nie wiem.
EN: I... I don't know. They wanted something... From me... From him... I don't know.
Geralt to himself

PL: Dziwne. Zupełnie jakby... Coś blokowało moje znaki.
EN: Strange. It's as if... something's blocking my signs.

PL: Mam wrażenie, że coś mi tu umyka. Zaraza...
EN: Feels like I'm missing something. Can't seem to put my finger on what thought. Damn it...

PL: Dużo przeszłaś. Odpocznij. Pójdę umówić spotkanie z Nilfgaardczykami, tak jak ustaliliśmy.
EN: You've been through a lot. Rest. I'll go arrange a meeting with the nilfgaardians as agreed.
I'll come back to get you later.

PL: Byłem w twoim domu. Znalazłem tam kilka rzeczy. Lusterko, amulet Melitele i twój pamiętnik. To nic wielkiego, ale jak na nilfgaardzką łączniczkę, to dość osobliwy zestaw.
EN: I went to look at the house where you were kidnapped. Found some of your belongings. The hand mirror, the amulet of Melitele, and your diary. Everyday items, as it were, but certainly odd things for a nilfgaardian informant to be carrying around. Can you tell me why?

PL: To... Och... Och, na bogów... Ja...
EN: These... oh... oh my... I...
You remember?

PL: Tak... Już pamiętam... Nazywam się... Merida... A on to... Helphyr... Nie... Arachos... Pająk...
EN: I... I do... I remember... My name... I'm Merida... And he... Helphyr... No... Arachous... The Spider...
That our masked benefactor?

PL: Tak... Helphyr. Nilfgaardzki mag z dworu Emhyra. Znany też jako Arachos.
EN: Yes... Helphyr - a nilfgaardian mage at Emhyr's court. Known also as Arachous.

PL: Niech zgadnę. Należy do kultu. Niech to... Mogłem się domyślić.
EN: Let me guess - he's part of the cult. Damn... should have seen it coming.

PL: To nie wszystko. On... On jest przywódcą komórki. Teraz pamiętam. Byłam jego pionkiem, narzędziem... Nie jestem Nilfgaardką. Jestem kapłanką Melitele.
EN: Not only that - he... he is a cell leader. Now I remember - I was his pawn, his tool. I am not a nilfgaardian. I'm a priestess of Melitele.

PL: Porwano mnie... Wykorzystano... Przemieniono w jedną z jego wiedźm ze względu na moje zdolności. Wiedział, że może użyć mnie do wzmocnienia rytuałów... Do robienia strasznych rzeczy.
EN: I was kidnapped... used... transformed into one of his own witches because of my background. He knew he could make use of me to empower his rituals... To do horrible things.
So those bite marks...

PL: Rytuał... Tysiące tych istot... Gryzły mnie... Miałam widzieć... Tamtego... Tamtego potwora...
EN: A ritual... thousands of those things... biting me... making me see... that thing... That monster...

PL: Myślisz, że po to cię porwali? Z tego co wiem, nie każda gałąź kultu Lwiogłowego Pająka ma kogoś takiego jak ty. Ludzi jak ty prawie się nie spotyka.
EN: Do you think that was the motive behind the kidnapping. Got a fleeting memory that not every cell of the Coram Agh Tera cult has someone like you. In fact people like you are hard to come by.

PL: Wydaje mi się, że nazywali cię Prorokinią Przeznaczenia.
EN: I belive they called you Prophetess of Fate.
Yes, i-it was horrible...

PL: Już po wszystkim. Nie masz się już czego obawiać.
EN: It's over now. You don't have to be afraid anymore.

PL: Byłam pod jego kontrolą... Nie wiem jak długo.
EN: I... was under his control for... I don't know how long.

PL: Czyli wykorzystał mnie, żebyś do niego wróciła, a przy okazji usunął rywalizującą komórkę. A ja tańczyłem, jak mi zagrał. Zaraza...
EN: So, he used me to get you back to him and eliminate a rival cell. And I played right into the palm of his hand. Damn...

PL: Tak... Ale jeszcze nie jest za późno... Tego człowieka... Tego potwora trzeba powstrzymać, wiedźminie. Proszę...
EN: Yes... but it is not too late... That man... that monster... must be stopped witcher. Please...

PL: Czy pamiętasz, co stało się, kiedy cię dopadli? Trafiłem na coś ciekawego, gdy szedłem twoim tropem. Walczyłem z przeklętą istotą...
EN: Now, do you recall what happened when they kidnapped you. Came across a curious scene while tracking you. Fought a cursed being as well...

PL: Nadal pamiętam jak przez mgłę, ale...
EN: My memory is still a bit blurry, but...

PL: To ja go przeklęłam. Chyba odruchowo, bez zastanowienia... Zmusiłam go, żeby mnie chronił, ale to ich nie powstrzymało.
EN: Yes, I cursed him. It feels like I did it by reflex, without even thinking... I forced him to protect me, but nothing came out of it. They still managed to drag me away.

PL: Hmm, to wiele tłumaczy. Byłaś wtedy pod kontrolą Arachosa. Kapłanki Coram Agh Tera władają potężnymi klątwami. Mój dawny przyjaciel, Nivellen, doświadczył tego na własnej skórze.
EN: Hmm, makes sense. You were under Arachous' influence then. Coram Agh Tera priestesses wield powerful curses. An old friend of mine, Nivellen, had experienced it first hand.

PL: Udało ci się zdjąć tę klątwę?
EN: Did you managed to lift it, master witcher?
No, I didn't. He did.

PL: Taki mam zamiar. Zostań tu i nie rzucaj się nikomu w oczy. Ja zajmę się resztą. Znajdę cię, kiedy wszystko się skończy.
EN: Plan to do just that. You should stay here, lay low. I'll deal with the rest. Will come find you when its all over.
Thank you... thank you...

PL: Błagam... Powstrzymaj Arachosa... Zanim znowu kogoś skrzywdzi.
EN: You... you have to stop Arachous... don't let any more people suffer.

PL: Wycisz swoje myśli. Jesteś bezpieczna. Czy na pewno nic sobie nie przypominasz?
EN: Calm your mind. You're safe. Are you sure you can't remember?

PL: Ja... Nic nie pamiętam. Czegoś chcieli... Ode mnie... Od niego... Już nie wiem.
EN: I... I don't know. They wanted something... From me... From him... I don't know.

PL: Hmm... To samo co z dopplerem, jakby coś blokowało moje znaki.
EN: Hmm... same as with the doppler. It's as if... something's blocking my signs.
Cage key
The Threadcutter
Note with instructions

Light the candles
Show your devotion
Touch the skull

PL: Arachos, ta? Już ci się to nie przyda.
EN: Arachous, huh. You won't be needing that anymore.
Master witcher...
Oh... gods. My head...
Janus?
You alright?
confused, he just regained consciousness

PL: Kto? Nazywam się Kolar. Ty... Ty mnie uwolniłeś, mistrzu?
EN: Who? My name... is Kolar. You... you freed me, master?

PL: Na to wygląda... Jego zaklęcie zostało złamane. Powinieneś już być sobą.
EN: I see. His control shattered. Well, you should be yourself now.

PL: Tak... Wielkie dzięki, mistrzu wiedźminie. Muszę... Muszę ruszać... Jak najdalej... Jak najdalej stąd.
EN: Yes... many thanks, master witcher. I... I must go now... Far away... From all of this.
On that we agree.
Let's go. I'll show you the exit.
I wonder, witcher, is your arrival here out of necessity - to bring down the evil that looms over this land? Or is it out of sheer curiosity of what I have to say?
A bit of both you could say. Definitely more of the latter, now that I see who was seeking me out in the first place...

Do I bring out a sense of distrust perhaps? I know you aren't very fond of our kind, and choose to support your nordling kinsmen in worldly affairs more often than you do us. This, however, is not about politics. It is much more than that.
Figured as much, the way you're dressed and all. Don't want to draw unwanted attention, domestic or foreign I imagine.
You are very much correct, witcher. I trust this will not be a problem for the matters at hand?
Depends on how you answer my questions.
Naturally.
What do you actually want from me?
The cult - what's it all about?
Have you been keeping tabs on me?
What's in it for you?
This cult... Seems more organized than I had originally thought. Who are they?
Who, or what are they, witcher? The who of the matter is not important, as they could be anyone, as you have seen - a lowly farmer, any elderly woman, or even soldiers brandishing the Golden Sun.
Mean to say this cult spreads beyond hierarchy and borders?
Indeed. Beyond even any banner or allegiance. This is the Coram Agh Tera - The Cult of the Lionheaded Spider.
Hmm, I remember now, heard mention of them in Vizima a few years ago.
Ah, so you aren't completely unfamiliar.
Yeah, had made quite the home for themselves in the sewers below the Temple District.
Only heard rumors of their presence there, didn't have the pleasure to meet them.
Yeah, had made quite the home for themselves in the sewers below the Temple District. Were up to all sorts alongside Salamandra, which is how I found them in the first place.
The cult was never known to shy away from making their presence known, even if that meant revealing more than what was desired of their plans. Stupidity is sometimes synonymous with pride, I fear.
Only heard rumors of their presence there, didn't have the pleasure to meet them.

Wouldn't have made a difference either way, when Nilfgaard took the city, we had located their base of operations under the Temple District. It was apparent that the place had long been swept clean by one thing or another.
Assume you've been watching me, wouldn't reach out about the cult if that weren't the case.
Perceptive.
Haven't noticed any nilfgaardian lackeys tailing me, so its either someone I've talked to that spilled the beans, or the only one equally keeping to themselves like you - the doppler.
Very perceptive indeed, master witcher. Janus has his uses, being in the right spot at the right time is one of them.
So, what'd you offer him? Dopplers don't generally work for anyone. Gold, land, protection from the witch hunters?
Does it really matter?
Not really, just curious. He could stab you in the back any chance he gets you know.
The best agents always come with their risks. However, being alive as long as I have in this field of work, you pick up a few tricks to keep those under you... obedient.
Hmm, if you say so.
Hard for me to believe you're here out of the goodness of your heart. What's it really to you if this cult goes on killing a bunch of northerners or not?
Let me put it in your line of thinking witcher.
Say you've been hunting a monster. A very old and dangerous one at that. Been on its trail for days, weeks, months even. As you track it down, narrow its path, cut off any escapes, it gets cornered.

Eventually, it will appear to strike as a last mean of defense. You will be prepared for that time. What you won't be prepared for however, is any other possible threat that may loom nearby. Focusing solely on one thing blinds you to the dangers of your surroundings, dangers from places you may have already moved through, thinking that no harm may come from a path once thread on.
Wanting to eliminate any future threats early so they're not a problem when you fully conquer the North?

Precisely, witcher. Cults like this one thrive in the chaos we bring amidst our wars. It is when they least expect someone to notice and meddle in their affairs. Well, they will not have the pleasure of thinking this way for long.
If I help you.
If you help me indeed, witcher.
Let's get to the point - what do you actually want from me?

What I want, witcher, is a capable individual with no allegiance... Someone that acts not based on orders, but on the basis of their own code and morals... Someone with the hindsight to know when a threat is far too dangerous to be left to grow beyond any collective capability.
Is this supposed to be flattering, by chance? Didn't ask for compliments.
Patience is... a virtue not present in all of us, I see. Very well then. I need a tracker. An expert. You fit the description, no?
Depends.

I seek an associate of mine, a woman by the name of Carilla. A while ago I sent her as a diplomatic envoy to Novigrad to strike a covert deal with one of the city underbosses for aid in transporting nilfgaardian goods to the less fortunate races of the city.

The nordlings' treatment of the elves and dwarves there does not come only in the form of verbal and physical harassment, but with restrictions and prohibitions to essential goods for their daily needs. We aim to correct that.
Not out of good will I wager.
Of course not. We aimed to forge an alliance with the gangs and get the respect of the elven and dwarven communities of the city, as to be offered less resistance when we inevitably reach Novigrad.
Killing two ghous with one strike.
Precisely, in your brutish way of putting it.
And? What about Carilla then?
She was kidnapped, witcher. By the cult.
And how would you know that?

I lost contact with her a few days ago. Sent out an informant of mine to investigate. The cult's whereabouts were being monitored previously in the area around the city and, from what I have been told, signs point to their involvement in this ordeal.

I suspect a local branch has formed in or around Novigrad. The task then, is as follows - I need you to find her, and, if the cult really is behind it as I suspect, erase their presence from the vicinity.
Quite the task. So what, want me to blindly walk in and go see what happened? What of your informant?

That's just it, my informant has also gone silent as of late. I need you to find him and see what he has gathered. Base your search on that information and continue from there. He was last posted in the village of Arette, east of Novigrad. His name is Mravis.
Right. Will see what I can dig up.
I thank you, witcher, for agreeing to this. It is truly of great importance.
Yeah, yeah, the greater good and whatnot. I'm not helping you because I find your motivations inspiring.
I know.
But, you are helping nonetheless. As such, you shall need this.
What is it?

A Seeing Stone. The cult cell you faced here was but a young upstart, still unaware of their true potential. This one, however, I suspect to be far more dangerous. They are known to make use of illusion magic to hide their presence and tracks. This will surely be useful to you.
Thanks. Let's hope it works.
I can assure you of that. Send Mravis back to me once you have found him and he has passed on what he knows to you.
Alright then. I should get going.
One more thing, witcher - Janus will be keeping tabs on you at times. Just letting you know, as a sign of mutual trust.
Not very trustworthy sending him to track my every move in the first place now, is it?
Maybe not, but I have no guarantee either that you will follow through with this. I like to take my chances.
I understand, I suppose.
Good. Once you have succeeded, go to the Southern Gate of Novigrad. There's a loose brick where you can leave a message to arrange our final meeting. And your reward, of course.
If you say so.
Good luck, witcher. You shall most certainly need it.
So, besides dealing drugs and being chased by the Eternal Fire, what can you tell me of their other hobbies?
Brutality, murder, sacrifice. I trust you are familiar with those.
Naturally.
Where they act, death follows, witcher. Nothing is beyond them, not if it means pleasing their God. Preventing that is of mutual interest, I wager.

PL: Zawierzywszy przeczuciu, a przy okazji przewertowawszy graty pewnej starej kobiety, Geralt trafił na wzmiankę o spotkaniu przy cmentarzu. Zasadzka wydawała się pomysłem niezłym, ale wiedźmin i tak zwykle robi po swojemu.
EN: Deciding to trust his gut, and rummaging through an old lady's belongings, as one does, Geralt managed to find out some information about a possible meeting at the nearby cemetery. Perhaps taking a look and setting up an ambush is in order? But hey what do I know, he usually has his own way of handling things that sometimes I don't understand...

PL: Geralt, zamiast chwycić się jedynego sensownego tropu, zdrzemnął się w domu staruchy i rychło wyleciał za próg. Los jednak lubi płatać figle i pewien leśny jegomość, wcale nie tak tajemniczy, jak zapewne sam o sobie myślał, skierował go ku miejscowemu cmentarzowi. Nie mając lepszego tropu, wiedźmin ruszył właśnie tam.
EN: Deciding to blow his only clear option to find some clues by taking a nap in an old lady's home, Geralt was swiftly bolted out and left to wonder what to do. As fate would have it, a totally not-so mysterious fellow out in the woods had pointed him in the direction of the local cemetery. Seeing as that was his only lead, the witcher followed suit.

PL: Choć położenie było marne, Geralt zawsze umiał wygadać sobie nieco lepszy los. Ta pożałowania godna hałastra bandytów, poruszona jego słowami, przystała pomóc, przez co walka stała się odrobinę mniej nierówna.
EN: Despite the unfortunate situation, Geralt always knew how to talk his way into a better one if nothing else. The sorry bunch of bandits, moved by the witcher's words decided to help out, making the fight a tad less unfair, even though it wouldn't have mattered either way.

PL: Nawet w takim zamęcie Geralt potrafił wyczuć strach, albo przynajmniej swąd strachu, bo zasrane gacie też swoje mówią. Jednego z pachołków odciągnął od walki, a resztę posłał tam, gdzie diabeł mówi dobranoc. Wygląda na to, że wiedźmin zyskał nowego kompana, co, trzeba przyznać, nie zdarza mu się często.
EN: Despite the unfortunate situation, Geralt always knew how to smell the fear in someone, or maybe it were just the poor sod's soiled trousers - either way, talking one of the bandit lackeys out of the fight, the rest met their swift end. Suppose the witcher made a new friend, a rarity for him!

PL: Geralt i ów trzęsący portkami obwieś, który jakimś cudem przylgnął do wiedźmina, ruszyli na pomoc. Dobytku ocalić się prawie nie dało, ale para uszła z życiem, a sam tchórz postanowił odpokutować i ruszyć z nimi.
EN: Geralt and the confused soiled bandit, who seemed to like tagging alongside the witcher, rushed to their aid and managed to assist the couple as best they could. Little could be done for most of their belongings and as such they were forced to leave as soon as possible. At least the scared ruffian had a change of heart and decided to go with them, as recompense. I suppose even cowards can have a change of heart - I wouldn't know of course!

PL: Przeszukując bałagan pozostawiony przez napastników, Geralt znalazł kilka osobliwych przedmiotów: lustro, naszyjnik i pamiętnik, wszystkie należące najwyraźniej do Carilli. Zamiast rozjaśnić sprawę, znaleziska tylko jeszcze bardziej ją zagmatwały.
EN: Looking through the mess left behind by the assaulters, Geralt found several items of note - a mirror, a necklace and a diary, all seemingly belonging to Carilla. Rather than shed light on the matter, it looks like it just made the situation even more confusing. Still, even trivial things at first may come in handy at a later time.

PL:
Po zdaniu relacji Brankowi, Geralt, nasz niezawodny i nad wyraz skrupulatny wiedźmin, otrzymał obietnicę zapłaty za swe trudy. Jak to jednak bywa w podobnych historiach, Branek uprzejmie poprosił go, by wrócił wieczorem, gdyż nagroda nie była jeszcze gotowa. Doskonale to znamy! Człek się spieszy, a potem ani się obejrzy, a już zapomniał o ogniu pod garnkiem i pół chałupy stoi w płomieniach.

Co osobliwe, chałupa istotnie stanęła w ogniu, i to bynajmniej nie przez niedbalstwo. Płomienie zdawały się aż nazbyt... celowe, jakby ktoś bardzo się postarał, by wyglądało to na przypadek. Życie Branka i Anezki zawisło na włosku...
EN:
After reporting back to Branek, Geralt, our ever-diligent witcher, was promised a reward for his efforts. Yet, as these things go, Branek kindly asked him to return in the evening - his payment wasn't quite ready. You know how it is! Rush too much, and before you know it, you've forgotten the stove's lit and set the whole place ablaze.

Oddly enough, the house was on fire - not from neglect, mind you, but in a manner that felt far too deliberate to be an accident. Branek and Anezka's lives were in danger...

PL: Zostawić Anezkę na żer dzikim psom, gdy obiecało się Brankowi mieć ją na oku... Nieładnie, Geralcie. Bardzo nieładnie.
EN: Leaving Anezka to a pack of wild dogs, after promising Branek you'll look after her... tsk, tsk, don't think you can come up with an excuse for something like that, Geralt...
This should be the one.
All right. Now we wait.
Begins excited but quickly changes to almost worried when he sees Geralt alone

PL: Och... Eee... Jesteś sam? Niedobrze, niedobrze...
EN: Oh... oh? You're alone? Not good, not good.

PL: Gdzie on jest, Janus? Wszystko już wiem. To koniec. Powiedz mu, że jak chce dopaść dziewczynę, musi najpierw spotkać się ze mną.
EN: Where is he, Janus? I know what this is all about. Its over. If he wants Carilla, tell him to come and get her.
Oh, you should've listened...

PL: Ślady pokrywają się w czasie... Ludzie szli obok niego? Brak oznak walki...
EN: This track is as old as the warewolf's... people walked alongside it? No signs of a struggle as well...
Anyone? Help us!
Someone! Anyone!
We're stuck! Someone! Help!
Please! We'll burn alive in here!
Need another plank?
Not yet.
Phew, alright.

PL: Coś taki umęczony? Cały dzień stoisz, a ja odwalam najcięższą robotę!
EN: Why do you sound tired? You've been standing there all day while I do the work!

PL: Boś ty ugułem zdolniejszy i do tego robotny! Racja, chłopaki?
EN: Eh, your most suited for the job! Right lads?
Aye.
Keep at it, boys.

PL: Spójrz na tych przygłupów. Jeden haruje, a reszta się opiera o szpadel.
EN: Look at those dimwits. One's working and the others are watching.
Typical nordlings...

PL: Zdaje się, że to jest to gospodarstwo, które niedawno kupił kapitan Peter Saar.
EN: This should be the one captain Peter Saar bought recently.
Wonder what happened?

PL: Tfu. Nordlingowie... Nawet własnego domu nie potrafią dopilnować, tylko go puszczą z dymem. Zwierzęta.
EN: *Ptooey* Nordlings... Can't even look after their own houses without burning them down.
Hey, what are you doing there?

PL: A co? Patrzyłem sobie na krowę...
EN: Huh? I was looking at the cow...

PL: Oglądać możesz, nic ponadto. Po śmierci właścicieli i braku dziedzica kapitan Peter Saar Gwynleve uznał tę ziemię za własność Nilfgaardu.
EN: Looking is as far as it goes. Since the passing of the owners of this place and no one to take over it, captain Peter Saar Gwynleve has claimed this plot of land state property of Nilfgaard.
The same goes for the cow, as well.

PL: Szok i niedowierzanie, kurwa jego mać.
EN: Course it does...
Did you say anything?
Nothing, nothing...

PL: Słyszę, że gadacie o jakimś nowo przybyłym. O co chodzi?
EN: Hey, heard you talking about a newcomer. What's this about?

PL: Ano, o naszym kowalu. Dziwny typ, szczerze mówiąc. Przyszedł pewnego dnia i powiedział, że chce "podszlifować swój fach". Tylko nie wiem, jaki fach chce szlifować, klepiąc podkowy przez cały dzień.
EN: Aye, 'twas the new smith in town. Weird bloke to be honest, came in one day and said he was "looking to hone his skills". Don't know what sort of craft he intends to master fixing horseshoes all day in a village out north.
Hmm, so where is he?

PL: Kilku chłopaków pognało go aż do tej jego chaty, tam, kawałek dalej. Ponoć fuszerkę odwalił, sprzęt im miał naprawić, a wyszło gorzej niż było. Oj... Będzie miał nauczkę, to pewne.
EN: Bunch of the boys chased him back to his hut over yonder. Turns out our smith did a lousy job fixing up their equipment, even got it back to 'em worse, by their accounts. In for a thrashing he is, for sure.
I see, thanks. Will check it out.

PL: A co ci do tego, mistrzu wiedźminie?
EN: What's it to you, master witcher?

PL: Powiedzmy, że potrzebuję go jeszcze przed lekcją życia, póki będzie w stanie odpowiedzieć na kilka pytań. Potem się zobaczy.
EN: Let's just say I need him breathing for what I'm about to ask him. Afterwards, we'll see.

PL: Tak czy siak, dobrze mu tak.
EN: Good riddance either way.

PL: Słyszę, że gadacie o jakimś nowo przybyłym. O co chodzi?
EN: Hey, heard you talking about a newcomer. What's this about?

PL: Ano, o naszym kowalu. Dziwny typ, szczerze mówiąc. Przyszedł pewnego dnia i powiedział, że chce "podszlifować swój fach". Tylko nie wiem, jaki fach chce szlifować, klepiąc podkowy przez cały dzień.

PL: Tak czy siak, dobrze mu tak.
EN: Good riddance either way.
Arachous
Trodo
Zamwell
Perry
Mippin
Cursed Being
Janus
Mravis

PL: Srebrny miecz należący niegdyś do wiedźmina Kolgrima.
EN: Silver sword once belonging to the witcher Kolgrim.

PL: Szabla należąca niegdyś do maga Arachosa
EN: Saber once belonging to the mage Arachous

PL: Skrawek rozdartej tkaniny. Materiał wygląda na kosztowny.
EN: A piece of torn cloth. The material looks expensive.
Letter found on a bandit's body.

PL: Starannie złożony list opatrzony nilfgaardzkim herbem
EN: Neatly folded letter bearing a Nilfgaardian insignia.
Plain gold ring. It looks precious...

PL: Hmm. Może pomogę im znaleźć ten pierścień.
EN: Hmm, maybe I can help them find this ring.

Geralt swims up to the fish, but someone hooks it out from right in front of him. He surfaces, looks around and sees a nobleman navigating his boat towards King Cormorant's isle. G yells after him, to no effect. G swims (or takes a boat) after the nobleman.
calling out to the nobleman as he sails away
Hey! Wait!
trying to stop the nobleman from sailing away
Stop! It's important!
Swam away with the ring.
Dammit! Gotta chase after it.
to himself, just swam to the surface of a river(maybe breathing a bit heavily at the start because of that), annoyed

PL: To chyba jakiś żart. Utopiec zabrał pierścień.
EN: Gotta be kidding me. Drowner took the ring.
Better go after it.
feverish
Geralt! What of Vivienne?
straight, did his job
Managed to lift her curse.
[PUP] try to avoid going high, in disbelief, confused
Truly? So all will be well, her troubles are done?
puzzled, a tinge suspicious
Guillaume. What--? What're you doing here?
drunk, desperate, angered
Leave me beвЂ¦ Without my Vivienne, life has no meaning. You are the last to whom I wish to speak.
Vivienne's happy.
No knowing how long she'll live.
She asked me to pass on a message.
straight
Vivienne chose what suited her best. She's completely free now. And happyвЂ¦ I think.
[PUP] try not go high, maybe more as a statement, disappointed
Without meвЂ¦
straight, speaking the painful truth
You helped her. But that doesn't mean she owes you her love.
[PUC] straight, corrected awkward wording
Method she chose worked. Thing is, she might only live for a few years more. Seven or so, most likely. Though I could be wrong.
angry
You had better be! You failed me, witcher. I placed my trust in you, and--
hard at fist, interrupting him, then softening a tinge
It was Vivienne's decision. Wanted to do it her way. Can't blame me for that.
straight
Asked me to give you a message - said she's grateful. Thanks to you, she can live a normal life.
[PUC] impatient
Good, goodвЂ¦ but how does she fare?
In Novigrad? What're you doing here?
How are you?
friendly, calm
Vivienne. Hey.
friendly
Witcher! So good to see you.
serious, professional curiosity
How do you feel? No relapse?
pleased, open
None. I feel wonderful. You've no idea how relieved I am. I went to see a mummers play in Pont Vanis. Out of doors, and it went on late into the night.
[PUC] pleased, reminiscing
And I did not need to flee at dusk. I also attended Baroness de Cordelle's ball, danced till the break of dawn!
friendly, relaxed chat
What's brought you to Novigrad?
delighted
I've toured the entire city, already. Now I seek a ship to take me to the Skellige Isles.
with curiosity, enthusiasm
I've heard there are beautiful flying sirens there. And mountains that rise out of the sea into the sky. Is any of it true?
straight
It all is, actually. Except you'll want to watch out for the sirens. As beautiful as they are dangerous.
[PUC] friendly

I shall be careful, I promise. Farewell, Geralt. Ah! One last thing. I would like you to have this. I kept it at firstвЂ¦ as a memento. But I think I'd prefer not to have anything to remind me of that time. Will you take it?
[PUC] straight
Course. Thank you. And take care.
trying to console the jilted lover
Got this bard friend, philosopher, too, I guess, amateur. He'd say, if you love somebody, set them free.
bitter
Do you know what, Geralt? You earned your reward, so take it. But keep your advice to yourself. Adieu.
Guillaume stands and leaves.
He's talking about the cult showing up

PL: Kroki. Hm. Czyli jednak się zjawili.
EN: Footsteps. Huh. They did show up after all.

PL: Nilfgaardczycy? To by nawet miało sens. Tylko po co?
EN: Nilfgaardians? Guess it makes some sense. Wonder why though?
a bit suprised at first

PL: Nilfgaardczycy? To by nawet miało sens. Tylko po co?
EN: Nilfgaardians? Guess it makes some sense. Wonder why, though?
intrigued
You're tormented. By what?
Beat. The hermit looks at Geralt tensely. She's tormented by a phantom and at first isn't certain that Geralt isn't just another specter.
If possible, it would be nice if the hermit pokes Geralt with a finger (to make sure he's a physical rather than ethereal being).
suspicious, hesitant
Forgive me, but I must ask directly - are you a man? IвЂ¦ I no longer trust my senses.
I'll try to help.
Let's talk about my pay.
Stop taking hallucinogens. Problem'll go away on its on.
straight
Fine. You seem sure something's out there. I'll see what I can do.
straight, getting down to business
Start by taking a look around.
a tinge sarcastic
You're taking hallucinogens, a steady diet. Stop and your specter problem'll go away. Farewell.
straight
This demon of yoursвЂ¦ can you describe it?
trying to speak coherently, but rambles a bit, mumbles
It is an enormous horse, its hoof beats pounding in the dark. It gallops to me, stops, staresвЂ¦ then runs on.
straight, conducting an investigation
Sure it's not just a wild horse, run-of-the-mill?
terrified, tormented
The creature is not of this world. When it gazes at me, a terrible chill pervades my soul.
superstitious fear, terrified even just thinking about the specter
It is evil condensed, its eyes black, voidвЂ¦ Each time it comes, terror grips me, I cease what I'm doing, still my breath and heart.
The brew - what's it made of?
What's the demon look like?
interested, as a witcher knows his herbs, a tinge dubious about the visions, still
What's your brew made of? Gotta wonder what's causing such strong visions. You drop in some mushroom? Chumbertub? Dancing dewlap?
gaining respect for G
Close. The common graytop. I see you know your herbs.
puzzled, growing dubious
Graytop? Variety's extinct, if I recall correctly.
serious
Nearly, but not quite. I was able to grow a few from dried spores. I'd hoped a brew of graytop would reveal this nightmare. I was not wrong.
considering
Far as I know, graytop produces some powerful side effects. Though it can, in fact, reveal the hidden, the immaterialвЂ¦
tortured tone
Yes, I see the evilвЂ¦ Better that than to know it is there, but not know where.
resigned, tormented
Witcher. Will you help me after all?
Yes.
No. I'm a witcher.
straight, with a hint of bitterness
Not exactly. I'm a witcher. Quite a few folk think I don't deserve to be classified as a member of the human species. Know some personally.
with hope, then resigned
A witcherвЂ¦? But no, nor can you help me.
straight
We'll see. Just tell me what the problem is.
straight, gentle
Yes. Meaning, I'm physical, no specter or vision, if that's what you fear. Don't trust your senses? Why?
[PUP] tormented by a phantom, pained, up the ante on the torment, please, more
A foulness, a demon or wraith, haunts me. Grants me no rest. It wants something, I sense this, but know not what. Each day it appears, tarries, then leaves. It's here now - there, beyond the trees.
interested, stating a fact
Don't see a thing.
to the point, sees no contradiction in his words
Of course not. It cannot be seen.
doubting, hesitant, emphasis on 'you'
Yet you see itвЂ¦?
"[PUP] straight, with emphasis on ""endure"" and ""eye"", up the ante on the fear and torment, more"
Only when I drink the brew. Which brings a pain to my head, nausea. But I endure these to keep an eye on the wraith, my tormentor.
straight, professional
Need to talk about how much I'll get paid, first.
calm
Is it gold you want?
straight, nothing will shock him
Orens or Novigrad crowns. Florens'll work, too.
calm, confident
I've none of any coin. I've nothing, in fact. JustвЂ¦ potatoes in the cellar. A good crop this year. Tend to this quickly, completely, and several sacks will be yours. Agreed?
offended
A king's ransom, that!
counting on making a deal
I feel we could agree, were you to go a few lower.
pleased to make a deal
Agreed. That many I can spare.
offended
Unthinkable. What good is your aid if I starve come winter? Take what I offer. or begone and leave me in peace.
Fine. Potatoes it is.
Don't need any pay.
straight
If that's the case, no pay required. Need those potatoes more than I do.
disarmed
Don't have a choice, I guess. Potatoes it is, but how many?
Branek said you invested in this land recently. Why here?
So long.

PL: Twój mąż mówił, że niedawno zainwestowaliście w tę ziemię. Czemu tutaj? I czemu teraz, kiedy Nilfgaard stoi wam niemal pod samym progiem?
EN: Your husband said you invested in this land recently. Why here? Why now with the Nilfgardians at your doorstep?

PL: Ach, mój Branek zawsze wierzył w swój 'kupiecki nos', jak sam to nazywa. Od dnia, w którym spotkaliśmy się na targu w Wyzimie, bez końca gadał o nowych sposobach na zarobek.
EN: Oh well, Branek always had faith in his 'merchant's wit', or so he calls it. Since the day we met at the marketplace in Vizima, he's been on and on with his ideas for new financial opportunities.

PL: Chodzi pewnie o Plac Solny. Chcesz powiedzieć, że połączyła was niewidzialna ręka wolnego rynku, a nie miłość od pierwszego wejrzenia?
EN: Salt Square, I'm guessing. Mean to tell me the invisible hand of the market brought you together, or was it love at first sight?

PL: Coś w jego gadaniu o przyszłości zrobiło na mnie wrażenie. Porządny, przewidujący chłop to dziś rzadkość.
EN: Eh, something about the way he planned out his future made an impression on a lass like me. A reliable man is hard to come by these days!

PL: Najsampierw, myślałam sobie, że z tych jego marzeń to nic, jeno same rozczarowania, ale jakoś na koniec zawsze wszystko wychodziło mu na dobre.
EN: Anyway, his dreams were going to bring nothin but disappointment, I said to meself, but they always did seem to turn out in his favor.

PL: Wygląda na to, że nie w tym przypadku. Albo coś zwyczajnie nie lubi kupców z dobrym węchem.
EN: Seems like some of his luck ran out though. Either that, or there's some monster that dislikes merchants.
So long.

PL: Dobrze. Rzucę na to okiem. Może znajdę jakieś ślady.
EN: Alright, I'll go have a look. See if I can't find anything.
Break a leg.
Alright. I'll go have a look.
Did you see or hear anything?
Branek said you invested in this land recently. Why here?

PL: Twój mąż mówił, że niedawno zainwestowaliście w tę ziemię. Czemu tutaj? I czemu teraz, kiedy Nilfgaard stoi wam niemal pod samym progiem?
EN: Your husband said you invested in this land recently. Why here? Why now with the Nilfgardians at your doorstep?

EN: Oh well, Branek always had faith in his 'merchant's wit', or so he calls it. Since the day we met at the marketplace in Vizima, he's been on and on with his ideas for new financial opportunities.

PL: Chodzi pewnie o Plac Solny. Chcesz powiedzieć, że połączyła was niewidzialna ręka wolnego rynku, a nie miłość od pierwszego wejrzenia?
EN: Salt Square, I'm guessing. Mean to tell me the invisible hand of the market brought you together, or was it love at first sight?

PL: Coś w jego gadaniu o przyszłości zrobiło na mnie wrażenie. Porządny, przewidujący chłop to dziś rzadkość.
EN: Eh, something about the way he planned out his future made an impression on a lass like me. A reliable man is hard to come by these days!

PL: Najsampierw, myślałam sobie, że z tych jego marzeń to nic, jeno same rozczarowania, ale jakoś na koniec zawsze wszystko wychodziło mu na dobre.
EN: Anyway, his dreams were going to bring nothin but disappointment, I said to meself, but they always did seem to turn out in his favor.

PL: Wygląda na to, że nie w tym przypadku. Albo coś zwyczajnie nie lubi kupców z dobrym węchem.
EN: Seems like some of his luck ran out. Either that, or there's some monster that dislikes merchants.
Anything of note that you may have seen or heard?

PL: Ano... Mój mąż to, prawdę mówiąc, ma słuch jak koń w burzę, więc jak go spytacie, to zaraz zacznie kręcić głową i mówić, że nic takiego nie było... Ale ja naprawdę słyszałam wczoraj hałas, tam przy naszych płotach. Brzmiało to bardziej jak ludzie niż jak jakieś bestie... Branek swoje gada, że to niemożliwe, ale ja wiem, co słyszałam. I nie wydaje mi się, żeby jakiekolwiek potwory umieli sklecać zdania!
EN: Well, my husband's senses are as dull as dirt to tell the truth, so if you ask him to confirm this he will nay in dismay, but I really did hear a ruckus last night, near our fences. Sounded like people more than some kind of beast... Branek says that can't be, but I know what I heard, and I don't think monsters can form sentences as of yet in this world!
You'd be surprised...

PL: Ty pewnie jesteś Anezka. Przysyła mnie twój mąż. Jestem Geralt. Mówił, że macie mały problem z potworem.
EN: You must be Anezka. Your husband sent me. Name's Geralt. Said you had a bit of a monster problem.

PL: A juści, mistrzu... Źle się porobiło. Tam, na polu leżą... Wszystkie mućki przez noc wybite. Co za plugastwo mogło takiej rzeczy dokonać?
EN: Aye master, aye. Quite unfortunate see, our cattle lay dead in the field over there. All of 'em killed in one night. What foul creature could do such a thing?

PL: Dla bestii bydło to łatwa zdobycz. Nie stawia się jak ich typowa przekąska.
EN: Easy pray as far as farm animals go. Not much resistance compared to the usual monster feed.
a bit confused
That being?
dry and to the point
People.
a bit scared

PL: Ach, tak... To ci dopiero okropieństwo!
EN: Oh yes... Horrifying.

PL: Twój mąż mówił, że niedawno zainwestowaliście w tę ziemię. Czemu tutaj? I czemu teraz, kiedy Nilfgaard stoi wam niemal pod samym progiem?
EN: Your husband said you invested in this land recently. Why here? Why now with the Nilfgardians at your doorstep?

PL: Ach, mój Branek zawsze wierzył w swój 'kupiecki nos', jak sam to nazywa. Od dnia, w którym spotkaliśmy się na targu w Wyzimie, bez końca gadał o nowych sposobach na zarobek.
EN: Oh well, Branek always had faith in his 'merchant's wit', or so he calls it. Since the day we met at the marketplace in Vizima, he's been on and on with his ideas for new financial opportunities.

PL: Chodzi pewnie o Plac Solny. Chcesz powiedzieć, że połączyła was niewidzialna ręka wolnego rynku, a nie miłość od pierwszego wejrzenia?
EN: Salt Square, I'm guessing. Mean to tell me the invisible hand of the market brought you together, or was it love at first sight?

PL: Coś w jego gadaniu o przyszłości zrobiło na mnie wrażenie. Porządny, przewidujący chłop to dziś rzadkość.
EN: Eh, something about the way he planned out his future made an impression on a lass like me. A reliable man is hard to come by these days!

PL: Najsampierw, myślałam sobie, że z tych jego marzeń to nic, jeno same rozczarowania, ale jakoś na koniec zawsze wszystko wychodziło mu na dobre.
EN: Anyway, his dreams were going to bring nothin but disappointment, I said to meself, but they always did seem to turn out in his favor.

PL: Wygląda na to, że nie w tym przypadku. Albo coś zwyczajnie nie lubi kupców z dobrym węchem.
EN: Seems like some of his luck ran out. Either that, or there's some monster that dislikes merchants.
uncertain, frightened, wants to help, but Geralt interrupts her
Geralt, I shall help you--
firm, but not rough
Step back.
a tinge annoyed, mostly determined, stubborn, preparing to fight
Stop telling me what to do!
gloomy, with satisfaction
You are stubborn, dh'oine.

PL: Jak ci się wydaje, co stało się Darkowi? Ta wiedźma rzuciła na niego urok, czy co?
EN: What d'you think happened to Darek? That witch curse him or somethin'?

PL: Skąd mnie to wiedzieć... Ale suka zapłaci za to.
EN: Dunno. But she'll pay for it.

PL: Mistrz jej potrzebuje, pohamuj się, Olek.
EN: Oi! Master needs her first. Calm your tits, Olek.

PL: Spkojnie. Mówię tylko co zrobię potem, ćwoku. Nie mów mi, że nie korci cię, żeby oglądać jak uchodzi z niej jucha.
EN: Course. I'm talkin' about after, you dimwit. What, you ain't itchin' to see crimson spill from her, Marek?

PL: Hah! Marnie się staracie, żeby to ukryć.
EN: Hah! Bastard's tryin' so hard to hide it.

PL: Kapłanki zapewne odchodzą od zmysłów. Masz tutaj kilka monet na drogę. I... Pozdrów ode mnie Nenneke.
EN: They're probably worried sick as is. Here, some coin for the trip. Say hi to Nenneke for me while you're at it.
You know Mother Nenneke?

Reference to Crossroads of Ravens, where we find out that the temple moved to Ellander due to some bad stuff happening. Geralt pauses and seems a bit sad for a moment remembering, but them goes back to normal

PL: Owszem, od kiedy sięgam pamięcią. Nawet zanim świątynia została przeniesiona do Ellander.
EN: Yes, for almost as long as I can remember. Even before the Temple... moved to Ellander.

PL: Mogłam się domyślić, zawsze umiała otaczać się dobrymi duszami. Jeszcze raz ci dziękuję, Geralt.
EN: I should have guessed, she always knew good souls to keep company with. Thank you again, Geralt.
Farewell, Merida.

PL: Powodzenia. Mam nadzieję, że jeszcze się spotkamy.
EN: And good luck. Hope we meet again.

PL: Żegnaj, Biały Wilku. Niech szczęście ci sprzyja.
EN: Farewell, White Wolf. Good luck on the path.

PL: Hm. Dużo się tu nie zgadza.
EN: Hmm. Something's not right.

PL: Dobrze. Rzucę na to okiem. Może znajdę jakieś ślady.
EN: Alright, I'll go have a look. See if I can't find anything.
Break a leg.
Alright. I'll go have a look.
Did you see or hear anything?

PL: Branek powiedział, że niedawno zainwestowaliście w tę posiadłość. Dlaczego właśnie tutaj?
EN: Branek said you invested in this land recently. Why here?

PL: Twój mąż mówił, że niedawno zainwestowaliście w tę ziemię. Czemu tutaj? I czemu teraz, kiedy Nilfgaard stoi wam niemal pod samym progiem?

EN: Easy pray as far as farm animals go. Not much resistance compared to the usual monster feed.
That being?
People.

PL: Ach, tak... To ci dopiero okropieństwo!
EN: Oh yes... Horrifying.
Anything of note that you may have seen or heard?

PL: Ano... Mój mąż to, prawdę mówiąc, ma słuch jak koń w burzę, więc jak go spytacie, to zaraz zacznie kręcić głową i mówić, że nic takiego nie było... Ale ja naprawdę słyszałam wczoraj hałas, tam przy naszych płotach. Brzmiało to bardziej jak ludzie niż jakieś bestie... Branek swoje gada, że to niemożliwe, ale ja wiem, co słyszałam. I nie wydaje mi się, żeby jakiekolwiek potwory umieli sklecać zdania!
EN: Well, my husband's senses are as dull as dirt to tell the truth, so if you ask him to confirm this he will nay in dismay, but I really did hear a ruckus last night, near our fences. Sounded like people more than some kind of beast... Branek says that can't be, but I know what I heard, and I don't think monsters can form sentences as of yet in this world!
You'd be surprised...

with a smile, diabolical

finishing his sentence, half smile
I was a bubbling, shapeless smear, having been rather spectacularly melted into a column of a certain castle.

doesn't quite know how to say it, awkward
I'm sorry. What happened - it was my fault. Never got a chance to apologize.
dismissing, with a smile

interested

Regis indicates the place where Dettlaff was before he disappeared.

friendly

with a sneer, pointed, a tinge sarcastic

Regis looks at the place where Dettlaff was before he disappeared.

sarcastic, but without malice

Regis points to the place where Dettlaff was before he disappeared.
[PUP] pron., straight
I came here for Dettlaff. I fear he's become entangled, landed himself in serious trouble.
calm
So that's his name. He's yourвЂ¦ friend?
[PUP] pron., calm, taking the round way about to his point, play the different beats, ending on a resolute note

with a sneer
Haven't exactly done a great job with that. He's killed one knight since I got here, least three others before I arrived.
[PUP] pron., resolute
For good reason, I'm sure. Understand, Dettlaff is not some decadent shit who kills for sport or to assuage a dryness of throat or a dullness of mood.
What good reasons could he possibly have?
How can you be so sure?
Don't give a damn about his motivations.
with distance, but interested
Seem pretty damn sure of that.
straight, confident
I am. Because I know him very well.
inquiring, a tinge challenging
So in your opinion, what're his reasons?
straight
Precisely what I wish to find out. And then I will convince him of the error of his ways.
piercing, does not share Regis' motivation or faith
Got a lot of faith in the guy.
[PUP] pron., straight
Vilgefortz melted my body. Dettlaff found what was left. As per our codex, he had a choice - to leave me where I was, or to care for me, nurture my remains.
storytelling
He chose the latter. Regenerated me at no small expense in his own blood. Do you know what that means to a vampire, the gravity of the endeavor?
straight
Probably same thing it means to a human. You owe him your life.
resolute
Much more than that. The act itself made us blood brethren, a bond so strong humans cannot even imagine. Which is why I know something ill is afoot.
calm, smiling a tinge
Always had an overdeveloped sense of empathy.
[PUP] pron., serious
Each vampire has a unique talent. One they hone over centuries. It's precisely what renders us so difficult to classify. Dettlaff's trump card is his herd instinct, his tribal propensity.
[PUC] straight
In point of fact, he prefers the company of lesser vampires and shuns that of humans. If he walks among you, killing egregiously, it can only mean something's upset him. Immensely.
Gotta find him.
Any idea what could've angered him?
straight, sarcastic
Gotta find him. Before something upsets him even more and all Beauclair is awash with blood.
with a sneer
Well, we share a cause, then. Just like the old daysвЂ¦
honest, meaning - if I have to kill him
Not entirely. I mean, when I find him, you know--
direct, candid
I know you've a contract on his head. Yet your true task is to stop the Beast killing, not necessarily to kill the Beast, am I right?
agrees, though unwillingly
All in all, sure.
"[PUP] pron., calm, the rational, measured one, ""only cows and elvesвЂ¦"" - saying repeated in preorder comic, Matters of Conscience"
Let us find him. By the time we do, I hope I'll have convinced you Dettlaff is no monster.
cross
Fine, all right already. But for now, evidence is stacking up against himвЂ¦
curious
Anything specific, some set of things that'd be likely to set him off?
[PUP] pron., considering, then explaining
How should I say thisвЂ¦ Dettlaff doesn't understand men, their world, its rules, its conventions. He's naГЇve, in a sense. He doesn't comprehend your games, knows not what it means to lie, deceive.
doubtful
Suggesting he's maladjustedвЂ¦ and venting his rage?
straight
I'm suggesting maladjustment can at times breed conflict. But is it the case this time? I cannot sayвЂ¦ but intend to find out.
feverish
Witcher! We flew here as fast as our coursers would carry us. Yet I fear we're late all the same! Pray, where is the Beast?!
calm
Still investigating, about to inspect this site. Withdraw your men before they trample all over the evidence.
serious, half announcing
Ahem, sirs! We must let the witcher do his work. Milton's murder cannot go unavenged.
The tension in the crowd drops, Folk lower their weapons, drop their heds, some leave.
The approaching pursuit mob is heard in the distance - barking dogs, muffled cries.
cautious, hurried
Hear that?
straight, hurried
The posse. Knights must've tracked me here.
hurried, firm
I'd prefer they not find me here. I've makeshift quarters at MГЁre-Lachaiselongue cemetery. We'll meet there.
agrees, hurried
See you.
Regis transforms into mist and disappears. A group fo angry tonwsfolk, guardsmen, etc., enter the warehouse.
hard
Everyone's got their reasons, but he's crossed a line. Want him to stop - don't give a damn why he's doing it.
philosophical
Perhaps you should. Motivations change the contours of all situations. A thief who steals to feed his family versus one who steals out of greed - would you not treat them differently?
[PUP] pron. Det-lahf, doubtful
Mean to suggest Dettlaff's killing out of some worthy, dire need?
straight
I merely suggest he's not a bloodthirsty monster who murders simply for the joy of it. That's just not him.
philosophical
Despite appearances to the contrary, you two are quite alike. You've both noble hearts yet you both are wont to perform ignoble deeds - when circumstances force you to, of course.
straight
Remember the year 964?
with a sneer
That was three centuries agoвЂ¦
unfazed
Blind fear gripped Rivia, Lyria and Spalla. Women and children were dying, their mutilated, dismembered corpses littered the fields.
remembering, straight
Brute of Lyria - read about it. Chewed up almost two hundred, then fell to a common poacher supposedly armed with a dagger blessed by some prophet--
[PUP] pron., interrupts, resolute
It fell to Dettlaff. Who then found a poacher asleep in the brush near his snares and dropped the fiend's corpse at his feet. And thus, a legend was born.
Why'd Dettlaff kill the monster?
Why tell me this? Any reason?
thinking, has doubts
Hm. Vampires rarely help humans. Must've had his own agenda, hunting the beast.
straight

mocking

resolute, a tinge scoffing

sarcastic at first, then doubtful, emphasis on 'need'
Awfully good of him. ButвЂ¦ do I need to know this?
straight

doubtful

guessing
